trajectory: user requested initial build after finalizing the design over discussion: frozen-policy in-context improvement, evidence-based argument contract, protected reward, lean (no state machine)
action: scaffolded framework — CLAUDE.md, /feature and /reflect rituals, worker/tester/reviewer agents, dispatch heuristics, reward boundary, decision log

trajectory: dry-run /reflect flagged CLAUDE.md '## Roles' duplicating agents/*.md (drift risk + always-loaded HOT bloat)
action: trimmed worker/tester/reviewer bullets to one pointer; agent files are the source of truth

trajectory: dry-run flagged the genesis entry referencing 'genesis' instead of the real baseline commit
action: set genesis commit to 4425239

trajectory: first-run onboarding, final form: flag must NOT be committed (else clones inherit done). Home = gitignored CLAUDE.local.md, which auto-loads into context, so per-clone AND no file-check. Committed CLAUDE.md holds only the stable instruction.
action: added /tutorial command; first-run offer is advisory via 'first_run_completed' in gitignored CLAUDE.local.md (dispatcher offers if absent, writes it on completion). No hook.

trajectory: user wants quality-first by default with reward-driven cost control — start strong, learn where cheaper is safe
action: set worker/tester/reviewer default model to opus; made model tier a dispatch parameter the orchestrator sets per task (Agent model override > frontmatter), downgraded per task-type only when reward shows opus is overkill (table in dispatch.md, filled by /reflect). Subsumes #4. Note: all-opus means worker and reviewer are no longer model-decorrelated.

trajectory: user: the dispatcher should learn to distinguish simpler vs harder tasks so it can pick the cheapest safe tier
action: made tier-learning explicit — dispatch.md infers difficulty from real outcomes (rounds-to-pass, tester failures, oscillation, escalations), downgrade-slow (>=3 clean cheap wins) / upgrade-fast (one struggle); /reflect maintains the tier table. No new log: transcripts + the dispatch table are the classifier.

trajectory: user: learning should be generally allowed and the framework must be aware it is adjustable
action: added a 'Self-adjusting' invariant to CLAUDE.md — adapting the framework (means) is the default (gradual auto + logged; big changes ask); only the reward is fixed. Makes self-adjustability explicit in the always-loaded file.

trajectory: user wants the observer (reward harvest) fired periodically — ~every hour of active coding
action: added read-only observer subagent (tools: Read/Grep/Glob, NO write — structurally cannot mutate the framework, so safe to auto-fire) + /observe command surfacing its summary; /reflect's harvest step now points at /observe. ADAPT stays human-gated — auto-firing the full self-edit would be the deferred autonomous adapter.

trajectory: user: add scheduling to fire the observer ~hourly during active coding (if possible)
action: wired a UserPromptSubmit hook (observe-timer.sh) that nudges to run /observe at most once/hour, only on real prompts (idle doesn't count). Chose it over /schedule cron (wall-clock + cloud, can't see the local session). It nudges (doesn't auto-run) and only the read-only /observe; adapt stays gated. UNVERIFIED: hook injection needs a fresh-session test.

trajectory: user set the git-autonomy boundary: dispatcher free on feature/dev branches, merges/pushes to main need user consent
action: added a 'Git workflow' policy to CLAUDE.md (a concrete consent-gate instance). Advisory for now — the human is present and the harness already gates commit/push; a deterministic PreToolUse guard is only needed for headless/unattended runs (offered, not added).

trajectory: user: make tier/escalation logic explicit in the driver docs; start effort xhigh, dispatcher adjusts per task. CC-guide check: effort has NO per-invocation override (frontmatter/session only), unlike model.
action: encoded 3 dials — model (per-task override, opus default), effort (xhigh default: worker/tester/reviewer=xhigh, observer=xhigh — its read IS the meta-reward signal, so max effort on signal-producers and trim only pure-execution; varies by role/session not per spawn), orchestration (cheap named pipeline default; escalate to ultracode/workflow on human-owned risk floor OR struggle OR breadth; both tiers same reward gate). Added 'Tiers & effort' to CLAUDE.md + detail in dispatch.md. Grounded: effort defaults high (safe upside), orchestration defaults cheap (adds failure modes — Agentless/MAST).

trajectory: user ranked the reasoning-critical nodes: dispatcher #1 (aims everything), observer #2 (learning-signal source); both must run at max capacity
action: observer pinned opus+xhigh in frontmatter (framework-controlled). Dispatcher = main session, framework cannot force its model/effort, so added a loud requirement to CLAUDE.md: run the session at opus + /effort xhigh. Rule: max capacity on the aimer (dispatcher) + signal nodes (observer/tester/reviewer); trim only pure-execution.

trajectory: user defined self-adjustment governance: observer (no priors, collects+processes reward) + dispatcher (has priors/doctrine) must agree to change any means; no consensus -> dispatcher decides
action: added 'Changing the stack' to CLAUDE.md — two-key data+doctrine consensus, dispatcher tiebreak BOUND by argument contract (evidence>authority, override logged), reward off-limits to both, big/irreversible -> human. Made observer explicitly doctrine-free + collect/process-reward in observer.md; stated dispatcher's effort/model/orchestration actions.

trajectory: user: skip permission-asking for harness changes (ask forgiveness; git reconstructs the harness anytime) — just require changes be incremental not revolutionary
action: dropped the human-permission gate for HARNESS/means changes (now auto by consensus, logged, git-revertible; discipline = incremental not revolutionary, the precondition that makes revert>ask). Preserved two non-reconstructable gates: reward off-limits to the loop, and outward-facing/irreversible WORLD actions (merge/deploy/destructive) still ask. Updated self-adjusting invariant, Improvement, Changing-the-stack, reflect.md.

trajectory: user needs to drop the framework into apps that already have a CLAUDE.md/.claude — submodule rejected (files must be at project root; framework is meant to diverge per-project, not be a pinned dependency)
action: added install.sh — non-destructive, idempotent merge installer (jq deep-merge of settings: union permissions + concatenate hooks; copy non-colliding agents/commands/hooks; fresh per-project memory; append CLAUDE.md under a marker; append gitignore). Tested against a project with existing CLAUDE.md/settings/worker.md — merge + collision-skip + idempotency verified. Documented in README.

trajectory: user: shared done-questionnaire; worker fills, reviewer answers BLIND, the worker-vs-reviewer DELTA is the real signal; observer (no-priors, closest to reward) prepares the score; orchestrator consumes just the delta-digest
action: added 6-field done-questionnaire (worker.md fills, evidence-backed; reviewer.md answers blind + flags divergences). observer.md gained per-task scoring mode (delta-digest + evidence-grounded assessment; proposes-not-decides; asymmetric: big delta=reliable 'not done', small delta!=done). feature.md wires it (dispatcher consumes digest, evidence+human gate, carry-delta-forward on fail). CLAUDE.md: observer in Roles, new loop line, aimer!=scorer in reward. Self-report is never the gate.

trajectory: user: add a reproducibility directive — testing must be based on real-life usage, and understanding+replicating real usage must be a FIRST activity (before building), not deferred to test time
action: added Directive 3 (understand real usage -> replicate it as the reward you build toward; tests-pass necessary-not-sufficient; honest about the gap; worker builds toward the check, doesn't author it). Wired real-usage-first into feature.md step 2. definition-of-done.md is deny-listed (human-owned), so the directive lives in CLAUDE.md not the reward file.

trajectory: user: all work should open with a 'metalanguage negotiation' — dispatcher presents anticipated work as a diagram/summary and aligns shared understanding with the human BEFORE building
action: made align-first the opening move of the loop (feature.md step 1): dispatcher presents a diagram + summary, negotiates scope/terms/what-done-means = the Query everything is later scored against; resolve ambiguity here (cheapest place); scale to task. Updated CLAUDE.md loop line + orchestrator role. Leans on Directive 1.4. Bookends with the end-of-loop questionnaire (align on Query -> score against Query).

trajectory: user: 'negotiation' is a metaphor for tightening the metalanguage at all levels; observer should assist the dispatcher in this phase (flag loose meaning + sketch E2E test flow); go/no-go gate — dispatch only once metalanguage is tight
action: reframed opening as an 'alignment phase' (feature.md step1 a/b/c): dispatcher diagram/summary + observer (no-priors) flags loose metalanguage and sketches the E2E real-usage flow (= per-task check, Directive 3); go/no-go before dispatch. Added alignment-assist as observer's 3rd mode -> observer now bookends the Query (tightens it at start, scores it at end). Updated CLAUDE.md loop line + orchestrator role; dropped 'negotiate' wording.

trajectory: meta-tier friction surfaced in the edge-c2 project: the dispatcher tried to promote 3 locally-harvested /reflect rules straight to the toolkit (source of truth) on a SINGLE project's observation; user: 'we can't guarantee data independence -> make a queue + vote, promote an outer candidate only when observed >2x'
action: added a cross-project upstream-candidate QUEUE + vote. New .claude/memory/upstream-candidates.jsonl (one entry per candidate with an observations[] vote-log; toolkit-only, not synced down). reflect.md step 3 now ROUTES changes LOCAL-vs-GENERIC: generic candidates are SUBMITTED to the shared queue by the DISPATCHER (the observer FLAGS them but never writes — preserving its read-only / safe-to-auto-fire invariant), and PROMOTED into the toolkit means only after >2 independent observations (>=3) across distinct projects/chats (cross-project analogue of downgrade-slow). Seeded edge-c2's 3 candidates (scope-ops-prior-art, log-task-outcomes, probe-past-bounds) at 1 observation each, status queued. Built on branch feat/upstream-candidate-queue for review (not merged to main / not pushed).

trajectory: user: parallelization — proposed estimate-tokens + tokens/min throughput to decide whether/how; asked for research
action: 4-cluster research: token-estimate is the WRONG gate (~30x same-task variance + systematic underprediction, arXiv 2604.22750; latency follows critical path not token sum, LAMaS). Added Parallelization policy to dispatch.md: WHETHER=structure (default-serial coding writes; reads/review parallel-free incl. running tester+reviewer in parallel; writes only on disjoint write-sets + no dep-edge + no shared resource + no improvised convention; engineer independence by scaffolding contracts serially first), HOW-MUCH=throughput caps width only (route width to sonnet/haiku, opus for dispatcher/synth; cap 2-4 writers; probe-slice/measure-live/cap-hard), native mechanism ladder (don't bolt on LangGraph/CrewAI), integration=single-threaded reduce + behavioral check even after a clean merge. Voting-by-competition for hard tasks. CLAUDE.md pointer + feature.md note.

trajectory: user: this is a meta-optimizer — encode anti-overfitting in the DESIGN; observe across different projects/datasets and aggregate as unbiased as possible (edge-c2 friction was exactly this — promoting on same-project repeats)
action: added Design invariant 'Meta-optimizer — generalize, don't overfit' to CLAUDE.md: projects are the datasets; project-LOCAL changes fit freely; a GENERIC toolkit change must generalize across INDEPENDENT projects (no-priors observer = unbiased detection; promote only on recurrence across >=N DISTINCT projects; same-project/session repeats correlated; count != independence -> prefer diverse projects). Points to the existing /reflect LOCAL-vs-GENERIC + upstream-candidates mechanism. Human-directed design principle.

trajectory: user: add /sanity-check (request-vs-reality investigation), run it AS a /feature; open Q resolved in the loop's own sanity-check: does it already exist/built-in?
action: ran /feature on it. Sanity-check verdict = REFINE: native read-only investigation exists (plan mode / Explore agent) but no native path challenges the PREMISE (request-vs-reality + Directive-1 failsafes + go/refine/stop). Added /sanity-check as a thin skeptical wrapper over plan-mode/Explore (not a reinvented investigator): parallel reads, evidence-cited, go/refine/stop verdict; wired into feature.md alignment + CLAUDE.md loop. Verified: registered as an available skill. Dogfood: the loop's own sanity-check caught the does-it-exist question.

trajectory: user: make the dispatcher an entrypoint (else 'dead md files'?). Verified mechanism via claude-code-guide.
action: sanity-check verdict on the request = REFINE. Agent-as-entrypoint IS possible (settings 'agent' field; model pins to the session; CLAUDE.md STILL loads for custom main-session agents — only built-in Explore/Plan skip it, so no doctrine orphaning) BUT the agent body REPLACES Claude Code's entire default system prompt — a real cost for marginal gain (CLAUDE.md already loads). Chose the leaner mechanism: pinned the dispatcher's max-capacity directly in settings.json (model: opus + effortLevel: xhigh), closing the 'framework can't force the dispatcher's model/effort' gap while keeping CLAUDE.md as doctrine and keeping CC's default system prompt. Files were never dead (CLAUDE.md auto-loads; commands/agents/hooks registered+firing) — the gap was advisory-vs-ENFORCED max-capacity, now enforced as the session default. Updated CLAUDE.md (two-nodes-at-max + effort bullet) + dispatch.md effort note. OPEN: settings model/effortLevel keys need a fresh-session confirm. Named dispatcher-agent entrypoint available as an option if stronger-than-advisory adherence is wanted (with the system-prompt-replacement caveat).

trajectory: human directive: incorporate the edge-c2 voice-TUI arc fixes into the toolkit + push + merge (consciously overriding the >=3-distinct-project promotion threshold on judgment that these are general framework hygiene)
action: PROMOTED 8 candidates into the toolkit means (generalized, anecdotes stripped): worker.md +Real-usage-test questionnaire item; reviewer.md mirrors it + scrutinize-hardest; tester.md probe-past-bounds incl temporal/stateful + assert-rendered-frame; dispatch.md += Everything-through-the-loop/decorrelated, Real-life-test-for-TUI/CLI, Escalate-on-oscillation, Honor-the-harvest-cadence(+log-outcomes), Stale-build & multi-agent ops hygiene. Candidate statuses set promoted. Still queued: scope-ops-prior-art, confirm-before-build, market-pred trio.
